## Lessons

### Lesson 1: Create your account

<!-- Article URL: /docs/lessons/create-your-account-and-table-of-contents -->

# Create your account and table of contents

<figure style="margin: 1.5rem 0;">
  <div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 55.172413793103445%; height: 0; overflow: hidden;">
    <iframe 
      src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/i0NCNTB_M8w" 
      width="580" 
      height="320" 
      frameborder="0" 
      allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" 
      allowfullscreen
      style="position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%;"
    ></iframe>
  </div>
  
</figure>

## What you'll learn

- Create and verify a new Hinto AI account.
- Set up a new project to house your documentation.
- Upload a video to automatically generate a table of contents.
- Generate individual articles from the AI-created structure.

## Creating Your Account

The first step is to create a free account with Hinto AI. This will give you access to the project dashboard where you can manage all your documentation.

1. Navigate to the Hinto AI homepage and click the **Get Started Free** button.
2. On the **Create Account** page, enter your email and a secure password.
3. Click **Create Account**.
4. Check your inbox for a verification email and click the **Confirm your email** button inside it. This will log you into your new Hinto AI account.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/e25927e80afeff156cb668bb902f88f3284f3ad2c88dcdb4d1e7847443b9f29f_a115bb87.jpg)

## Setting Up Your First Project

Once your account is verified, you'll land on the **Projects** page. A project is a container for a specific knowledge base, like documentation for a particular product or feature. Let's create one now.

1. Click the **Create Your First Project** button.
2. Give your project a descriptive name. In the lesson, we use "NoteTaker" as an example.
3. Click **Create Project**.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/1317189dc63db0269a6c722f46d5a9bd263495f9e99d2610b1d98ae51efd7192_0a5af102.png)

## Generating the Knowledge Base Structure

Hinto AI can generate a single article or an entire knowledge base structure from your video. For comprehensive documentation, starting with the **Knowledge base** option is recommended as it allows the AI to understand the broader context of your product.

1. In the 'Select a template' pop-up, choose the **Knowledge base** option.
2. Click the **Upload** button and select the product demo video from your computer.
3. Hinto AI will now analyze your recording to create a structured table of contents. This may take a minute or more.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/bb065320d9dbf5b19151da4733a7b9191df097963c4f8d3ffdcfc27f8594f329_48ef8782.png)

## Reviewing and Generating Articles

After the analysis, Hinto AI presents a complete table of contents with suggested article titles organized into sections. You can review this structure and then generate the content for each article one by one.

1. Once the process is complete, you'll see a pop-up that says 'Ready for Review'. Click **View**.
2. Examine the generated article list on the left. You can rename or delete any suggestions as needed.
3. Select the first article in the list. On the right, confirm the video and template are correct, then click **Generate**.
4. While one article is generating, you can click on the next article in the list and start its generation process.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/684aeec04802fd8b23803e0e17edfd8f2e7dce3581e92025dfd00180414ea179_71ff7bc3.png)

## Now you try

Practice what you've learned by creating your own knowledge base from a short screen recording. You can record a simple process, like how to create a new folder on your desktop.

1. If you haven't already, sign up for a Hinto AI account.
2. Create a new project named "My First Test".
3. Choose the **Knowledge base** option and upload your screen recording.
4. Review the generated table of contents. Does it accurately reflect the steps in your video?
5. Generate the first two articles from the list.

## Summary

In this lesson, you learned the end-to-end process of turning a single product demo into a fully structured knowledge base. By creating a project, uploading a video, and letting Hinto AI generate the table of contents, you can rapidly build the foundation for your help documentation and then generate detailed articles with just a few clicks.

## Related

- [Difference between a Knowledge Base and a Single Article](/docs/generating-content/difference-between-a-knowledge-base-and-a-single-article)
- [Generate a stand-alone article from a video](/docs/generating-content/generate-a-stand-alone-article-from-a-video)
- [Record a Product Demo for Knowledge Base Generation](/docs/lessons/record-a-product-demo-for-knowledge-base-generation)

---

### Lesson 2: Record a Product Demo

<!-- Article URL: /docs/lessons/record-a-product-demo-for-knowledge-base-generation -->

# Lesson:&nbsp;Record a Product Demo for Knowledge Base Generation

<figure style="margin: 1.5rem 0;">
  <div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 55.172413793103445%; height: 0; overflow: hidden;">
    <iframe 
      src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/A8joZTvseYc" 
      width="580" 
      height="320" 
      frameborder="0" 
      allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" 
      allowfullscreen
      style="position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%;"
    ></iframe>
  </div>
  
</figure>

## What you'll learn

- Understand how to narrate a product walkthrough for AI processing.
- Identify key UI elements to highlight during a recording.
- Learn best practices for creating a clear and effective demo video.
- Follow the process of uploading your video to Hinto AI to generate articles.

## Step 1: Plan Your Product Walkthrough

Before you start recording, have a clear idea of what you want to show. You don't need a detailed script, but you should know which feature you'll be focusing on. The goal is to create a simple, focused demo that clearly explains one part of your product.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/b966b3fcf2fa55c3c3f982eb74d0b23b39e9fd652e02410cec0d4593cccb01b9_786acb14.jpg)

## Step 2: Record and Narrate Your Demo

As you record your screen, narrate your actions as if you're explaining it to a new user. Speak clearly and at a natural pace. The most important thing is to describe what's on the screen and what you're doing. Don't worry about being perfect—Hinto AI is designed to understand natural speech, including pauses and small mistakes.

- Start by introducing the feature you're about to demonstrate.
- Clearly name UI elements as you interact with them, such as "the **Sort by** menu" or "the **New Meeting** button."
- Explain the purpose of each function as you click through the interface.
- Walk through a common user action from start to finish, like creating a new item or changing a setting.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/b72adf454cbf696a98cb6dea105e8b09c0d0561c1884b7c2d7c17e90199c370d_51126bbb.jpg)

## Step 3: Upload Your Recording to Hinto AI

Once you've finished recording and saved the video file to your computer, you're ready to turn it into documentation. Log in to your Hinto AI account and navigate to the project where you want to add the content.

1. From your project dashboard, choose the option to generate content from a video.
2. In the pop-up window, click the **Upload** button or drag and drop your video file.
3. Select the screen recording you just created and confirm the upload.

Hinto AI will then process your video and audio, identify the steps you took, and generate a complete set of draft articles for your knowledge base.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/b27ff824241b636963d63f7fa4b3852145cc0996cb85af5081feb29c1b780951_d6815b37.jpg)

## Now you try

It's time to apply what you've learned. Create your own product demo by following the techniques demonstrated in this lesson.

1. Choose a feature from your own software or a website you use often.
2. Record a short (1-2 minute) video where you explain how that feature works.
3. As you record, clearly narrate your actions, naming buttons and menus as you click them.
4. Save the video, upload it to a Hinto AI project, and review the articles it generates.

## Summary

In this lesson, you learned that creating an effective product demo for Hinto AI is straightforward. By clearly narrating your actions and walking through a feature, you provide the AI with all the information it needs to generate comprehensive help articles. Remember that a perfect delivery isn't necessary; clarity is the most important factor.

## Related

- [Generate a complete Knowledge Base from a video](/docs/generating-content/generate-a-complete-knowledge-base-from-a-video)
- [Generate a stand-alone article from a video](/docs/generating-content/generate-a-stand-alone-article-from-a-video)
- [Choose the Best Template for Your Content](/docs/generating-content/choose-the-best-template-for-your-content)

---

### Lesson 3: Your first knowledge base

<!-- Article URL: /docs/lessons/make-your-first-table-of-contents-and-knowledge-base -->

# Lesson: Create your first table of contents and product manual&nbsp;

<figure style="margin: 1.5rem 0;">
  <div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 55.172413793103445%; height: 0; overflow: hidden;">
    <iframe 
      src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/NfHLUvua4z0" 
      width="580" 
      height="320" 
      frameborder="0" 
      allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" 
      allowfullscreen
      style="position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%;"
    ></iframe>
  </div>
  
</figure>

## What you'll learn

- Upload a product demo to automatically generate a knowledge base structure
- Understand how Hinto AI analyzes video to suggest relevant articles
- Generate multiple articles simultaneously using different content templates
- Manage and refine the AI-suggested article structure before generating content

In this tutorial, you'll learn how to transform a single product demo video into a complete, structured knowledge base. Hinto AI analyzes your recording, proposes a logical article hierarchy, and generates the content for each article, turning hours of writing into minutes of review.

## Step 1: Upload Your Video Demo

The first step is to provide Hinto AI with your source material. You can use any screen recording, such as a product walkthrough, a customer support session, or an internal training video.

1. From your project dashboard, click **Add content...**
2. In the pop-up window, click **Upload** to open your file browser.
3. Select the video file you want to use. Hinto AI will immediately begin uploading and analyzing your recording to understand its content and structure.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/afcd41bd34387fd6b2fd8fbf703c392f4054dd5e6ea902beec85db3d99055b39_819734af.jpg)

## Step 2: Review and Refine the AI-Generated Structure

Once the analysis is complete, Hinto AI will present a suggested knowledge base structure in the left sidebar. This is a tree of article titles that logically breaks down the topics covered in your video. Before generating the content, you can refine this structure.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/fe36d6037617bd48c03920f1952179705cdb6ef63bfcceea300010457af232b1_021cae0d.jpg)

- **Reorder articles**: Drag and drop any article to change its position in the hierarchy.
- **Rename articles**: Click the three-dot menu next to an article and select **Rename** to give it a more precise title.
- **Delete articles**: If a suggested topic isn't relevant, click the menu and choose **Delete** to remove it.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/95fee38ab2ae12928427bf93e3f4f88a7199a653564c6c3e4d660f15039f5801_d62d1875.jpg)

## Step 3: Generate Your Articles

With your structure finalized, you can now generate the content for each article. You can kick off the generation for multiple articles in parallel, and Hinto AI will work on them in the background.

1. Select an article from the sidebar that is ready to generate.
2. Choose a **Template** that best matches the article's purpose, such as a "Basic guide" or "Feature overview."
3. Click the **Generate** button.
4. Repeat this for other articles in your structure. The system will notify you as each one is completed.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/1b05e641b8679efb89eb96e53b56c8b48ad22ff726712dd2b27e4c9e0c5e952d_707b352e.jpg)

## Now you try

It's time to apply what you've learned. Find a short screen recording of your own product and use it to create your first AI-generated knowledge base.

1. Choose a product demo or walkthrough video that is 1-3 minutes long.
2. Upload it to a new Hinto AI project and wait for the analysis to complete.
3. Review the suggested article structure. Try renaming one article and deleting another to practice refining the hierarchy.
4. Generate at least two articles using two different templates (e.g., "Detailed how-to guide" and "Feature overviews").
5. Review the generated content and, if a screenshot was created, use the **Crop** tool to adjust its focus.

## Summary

In this lesson, you learned the end-to-end process of transforming a single video into a fully structured, multi-article knowledge base. By leveraging Hinto AI's ability to analyze video, propose a logical article hierarchy, and generate content drafts, you can significantly accelerate your documentation workflow and create comprehensive help centers with minimal effort.

## Related

- [How to Record a Product Demo for Knowledge Base Generation](/docs/lessons/record-a-product-demo-for-knowledge-base-generation)
- [Generate a stand-alone article from a video](/docs/generating-content/generate-a-stand-alone-article-from-a-video)
- [Choose the Best Template for Your Content](/docs/generating-content/choose-the-best-template-for-your-content)

---

### Lesson 4: Publish your project

<!-- Article URL: /docs/lessons/publish-your-project-with-a-public-url -->

# Publish your project with a public URL

<figure style="margin: 1.5rem 0;">
  <div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 55.172413793103445%; height: 0; overflow: hidden;">
    <iframe 
      src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/BYsVoNAFytA?" 
      width="580" 
      height="320" 
      frameborder="0" 
      allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" 
      allowfullscreen
      style="position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%;"
    ></iframe>
  </div>
  <figcaption style="text-align: center; margin-top: 0.5rem; color: #666;">watch this&nbsp;</figcaption>
</figure>

## What you'll learn

- Publish your entire project to a live, shareable URL with a single click.
- Customize your documentation's appearance, including the URL slug, hero section, themes, and colors.
- Configure settings for search engine indexing and site navigation.
- Update your live documentation easily after making edits to your articles.

## Publishing Your Documentation

Once your knowledge base is ready, you can make it accessible to your users by publishing it to a public URL. This process is straightforward and can be done in just a few steps.

1. Click the three-dot menu next to your project name to open the **Project Settings**.
2. In the settings modal, the **Publishing** section is shown by default.
3. Click the **Publish Documentation** button to make your site live.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/1eb4f82773788d7334a12f9b9c14e3337eaeef8d62f73d8b5d29030f2c54d4b0_df6f1158.jpg)

After publishing, the status will change to **Published**, and you will see options to **View Live Site** or **Unpublish**.

## Customizing Your Public Site

Hinto provides a variety of options to customize the look and feel of your published documentation, ensuring it aligns with your brand. These settings are all found within the **Project Settings** modal.

- **General:** Customize your **URL Slug** to create a branded link. You can also toggle **Search Engine Indexing** to control if your site appears in search results.
- **Hero Section:** Edit the **Title**, **Subtitle**, and **Description** that appear at the top of your documentation's homepage.
- **Themes & Colors:** Choose between light and dark modes, upload custom logos, set your brand's primary and secondary colors, and select from a variety of fonts.
- **Navigation:** Add custom links to your site's header and footer to direct users to other resources.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/b5f00221ea22882b28fa2a99e6d652c43f677cd950a8ba190e8e9cff03e13e13_946585c1.jpg)

## Updating Your Live Documentation

As you make changes to your articles, you'll need to push those updates to your live site. Hinto makes this process simple.

1. After editing an article, click the **Save changes** button at the top right.
2. A **Republish** button will appear in the left sidebar under the published link.
3. Click **Republish** to update your live documentation with the latest changes.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/440b315b12714619367a6ef4f7d7b88298eb41abc90e0a270cad5d2f2ad70804_9775bb77.png)

## Now you try

Apply what you've learned by publishing and customizing your own project.

1. Open one of your projects and navigate to **Project Settings**.
2. Click **Publish Documentation**.
3. In the **Themes & Colors** tab, choose a new **Primary Color** that matches your brand.
4. Click **View Live Site** to see your customized documentation.
5. Return to the editor, make a small change to any article, save it, and click the **Republish** button to see your update go live.

## Summary

In this lesson, you learned how to publish your knowledge base and make it available to the world. You also explored the powerful customization options that allow you to tailor the URL, appearance, and navigation of your public site to create a professional, on-brand experience for your users. Finally, you saw how easy it is to keep your documentation current with the one-click republish feature.

## Related

- [How to Update Your Live Documentation](/docs/publishing-documentation/how-to-update-your-live-documentation)
- [How to Customize Your Documentation's Appearance (Themes, Colors, & Fonts)](/docs/publishing-documentation/how-to-customize-your-documentations-appearance-themes-color)
- [Configure SEO and analytics for your public documentation](/docs/publishing-documentation/configure-seo-and-analytics-for-your-public-documentation)

---

### Lesson 5: Use LLMs.txt with ChatGPT

<!-- Article URL: /docs/lessons/use-your-llmstxt-with-chatgpt-and-other-ai-tools -->

# Use Your LLMs.txt with ChatGPT and Other AI Tools

<figure style="margin: 1.5rem 0;">
  <div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 55.172413793103445%; height: 0; overflow: hidden;">
    <iframe 
      src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/I4jptDTG0B8" 
      width="580" 
      height="320" 
      frameborder="0" 
      allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" 
      allowfullscreen
      style="position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%;"
    ></iframe>
  </div>
  
</figure>

## What you'll learn

- Export your entire knowledge base into a single, AI-ready LLMs.txt file.
- Provide comprehensive product context to AI tools like ChatGPT in one simple step.
- Generate marketing materials, such as press releases or social media posts, using your documentation.
- Apply this technique to create various types of content based on your product's features and functionality.

## Exporting Your Knowledge Base for AI

Your Hinto AI knowledge base is more than just documentation for users; it's a comprehensive source of truth about your product. The LLMs.txt export feature consolidates all your articles into a single text file, perfectly formatted for large language models (LLMs) to understand. This allows you to leverage your existing documentation to create new content effortlessly.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/05df56e49edd7af0af76f5c2d74e6e4e6ced4bace2f6da907fb6d2b8b53b88bc_56f650c1.jpg)

1. From your project dashboard, open the **Project Settings**.
2. Navigate to the **Export Project** section in the left-hand menu.
3. Locate the **Export LLMs.txt** option and click the **Download** button.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/b761009224cc99cba7eccb416416804cb6be78e96fff04ef08c050e7c50d5624_07c5c74d.jpg)

## Providing Context to ChatGPT

One of the biggest challenges when using AI tools is providing enough context about your product. Instead of manually writing lengthy descriptions, you can simply upload the LLMs.txt file. This instantly gives the AI a deep understanding of your product's features, benefits, and how it works.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/7a8119d4298d4f6bd4433b32ac3b1e84e3d918cd6c6617ad59d2ef11636c54cd_6c24a3a4.jpg)

Simply open a tool like ChatGPT and drag your downloaded **LLMs.txt** file into the chat window. The tool will process the file, and you can immediately start asking it to perform tasks.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/60d21002c5e3d000dee23da1b48ae8a6d8b5feca11b41d558ae8ba9e0f3ec08e_13dccb65.jpg)

## Generating New Content

With the context provided, you can now ask the AI to generate a wide range of content. In this lesson's example, we ask it to "create an amazon style press release for my product hinto." Because the AI already has all the necessary information from the file, it can produce a detailed and accurate press release without any further input.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/555f4b2f34d307989e14ca5d89027529054216f4ee2a9dd20667c24721efbcfe_9d8794b5.jpg)

This method can be used to create content for various purposes, including marketing campaigns, social media updates, technical briefs, or even optimizing app store descriptions.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/eb14c381a84e5e5f4318c1d17f7c8866ead75729e7f7990b9b25ee3c09c96a34_ee6d7cdc.jpg)

## Now you try

It's time to apply what you've learned. Use the LLMs.txt file from your own Hinto project to generate a different type of content.

1. Export the **LLMs.txt** file from one of your projects.
2. Upload it to ChatGPT or another AI tool.
3. Ask the AI to "Create a 5-tweet thread announcing a new feature for [Your Product Name] based on the provided documentation."
4. Try another prompt: "Write a short blog post explaining the top 3 benefits of using [Your Product Name]."

## Key takeaways

In this lesson, you learned how to export your entire Hinto knowledge base into a single LLMs.txt file. This powerful feature allows you to instantly provide complete and up-to-date context about your product to AI tools, enabling you to generate accurate marketing copy, technical content, and more with minimal effort.

## Related

- [Export content for AI models (LLMs)](/docs/exporting-content/export-content-for-ai-models-llms)
- [LLMs.txt for Published Websites](/docs/publishing-documentation/llmstxt-for-published-websites)
- [What is Hinto AI and how it works?](/docs/getting-started-with-hinto-ai/what-is-hinto-ai-and-how-it-works)

---

### Lesson 6: Analyzing User Interviews

<!-- Article URL: /docs/lessons/lesson-6-analyzing-user-interviews -->

# Lesson 6: Analyzing User Interviews

<figure style="margin: 1.5rem 0;">
  <div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 55.172413793103445%; height: 0; overflow: hidden;">
    <iframe 
      src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/raQpj6oQfvQ" 
      width="580" 
      height="320" 
      frameborder="0" 
      allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" 
      allowfullscreen
      style="position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%;"
    ></iframe>
  </div>
  
</figure>

## What you'll learn

- Understand the user research analysis workflow in Hinto
- Create a Customer Interview project and provide essential research context
- Interpret the different types of reports Hinto generates from your interviews

## Understanding the Research Workflow

Hinto automates user research analysis through a structured, three-step process. This lesson will guide you through how to provide the right inputs to get powerful, aggregated insights quickly.

1. **Input Your Materials:** Start by gathering your research materials, which include your initial **Hypotheses** and the recorded **User Interviews**.
2. **Evidence Mapping:** After you upload your interviews, Hinto's AI analyzes each one individually. It maps evidence from the conversation back to your hypotheses, confirming, mentioning, or rejecting them for each participant.
3. **Generate an Insights Report:** Finally, Hinto aggregates the findings from all interviews to create a comprehensive insights report. This summary highlights key findings, validated hypotheses, and recommendations, saving you hours of manual synthesis.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/8bd4f14f54c42ac446849597a2c3d5e2eb18dbe7c008eb9bf6582372c0443470_1f40ebb1.jpg)

## Setting Up Your User Interview Project

Let's begin by creating a dedicated project for your user research. This organizes your interviews and analysis in one place.

1. From your main dashboard, click the **Create Project** button.
2. Select the **Customer Interviews** template. This template is specifically designed for analyzing user research.
3. Give your project a descriptive **Project Name**, such as "Video-to-Doc Setup Friction Study."

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/3c33af3ae2b1891bf53f119fcb7879a7a77a25386c03a095cfb4afba20f718e9_faf9f3fc.jpg)

## Defining Your UX Research Context

Providing context is the most critical step for getting accurate and relevant insights. The **UX Research Context** window prompts you to input the core elements of your study. This information guides the AI to focus on what matters most.

- **Business Problem:** Describe the core issue you are investigating.
- **Research Goals:** List the objectives and what you aim to learn from the study.
- **Hypotheses:** List the assumptions and beliefs you are testing.

You can easily copy and paste this information from an existing research plan document. Once you've filled it out, click **Save Context**.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/98c0b87a9f45ea3e491d8944819b8e30cd4d55ad7dcbadd1d3d9b1fcc0c8fd73_58605906.jpg)

## Analyzing Interviews and Reviewing Reports

After setting the context, upload your video files. Hinto will process them and generate a set of detailed articles. You'll find a folder for each interview containing topic-specific analyses and a final research report for that individual. Most importantly, Hinto creates a top-level **Hypothesis Validation Summary**, which aggregates insights from all interviews into one powerful document. This summary is your primary deliverable, perfect for sharing with stakeholders and making data-driven decisions.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/8dac3b13244efb0ca62e69f60c3abdd4d4a7dc242628b9aed4a2730890b6a8a1_9a6f9c37.jpg)

## Now you try

Practice what you've learned by creating your own customer interview project.

1. Create a new project using the **Customer Interviews** template.
2. In the **UX Research Context** window, write a sample business problem, a research goal, and at least two hypotheses you'd want to test.
3. Upload a short test video to see how Hinto generates the analysis reports.
4. Review the generated **Hypothesis Validation Summary** and the reports for your individual interview.

## Summary

In this lesson, you learned how to use Hinto to streamline your user research analysis. By setting up a dedicated project, defining a clear research context, and uploading your interviews, you can automatically generate both individual analyses and a powerful, aggregated insights report. This process transforms raw interview data into actionable findings, helping you validate hypotheses and improve your product faster.

## Related

- [Set Up a Customer Interview Project and Define Research Context](/docs/analyzing-user-interviews/set-up-a-customer-interview-project-and-define-research-cont)
- [How to Analyze a User Interview Recording](/docs/analyzing-user-interviews/how-to-analyze-a-user-interview-recording)
- [Understand the Aggregated Hypothesis Validation Summary](/docs/analyzing-user-interviews/understand-the-aggregated-hypothesis-validation-summary)

## Translating and Localizing Content

### How to Translate Your Knowledge Base into Multiple Languages

<!-- Article URL: /docs/translating-and-localizing-content/how-to-translate-your-knowledge-base-into-multiple-languages -->

# How to Translate Your Knowledge Base into Multiple Languages

This article explains how to automatically translate your articles into different languages. Following these steps will help you create a multilingual knowledge base to support a global audience. You will need administrator or editor access to the project.

## Add and Manage Translation Languages

1. From the article editor, click the **language dropdown** at the top of the page.
2. Select **Add Languages** from the menu. This will open the **Project Settings** modal.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/1d4aed09936268a426e6043f722000b8bb3f0b83d038b3c525cd1d14a248a720_d72fff5e.jpg)

3. Under the **Translated Languages** section, use the **Select language...** dropdown to choose your target language.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/1b34f146f42aaea8238c19a50817dbf867c95f2cacd3f6845ad8d27a6693abb8_9b6bc1d4.jpg)

4. Click the **Add** button. The system will automatically begin translating all articles in your project into the selected language. A progress indicator will appear.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/b8a0855452e0ae465373c13beeeebda913fe9363a0e5f7b48ed91284b97bad3f_22b5fd9f.jpg)

5. To remove a language at any time, click the **trash can icon** next to it and confirm the deletion.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/158ff0c1eeec7a3a274f557ea0a10538cf93d61dbd0ab846fbe62b87053def42_d50025a2.jpg)

## Viewing and Publishing Translations

1. Once the translation is complete, close the **Project Settings** modal.
2. Use the **language dropdown** in the editor to switch between the original and translated versions of your article.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/0bc1c674804248e704a8ae85ea41d6792e15511501ece36be87acd0dfbd7e4e9_d3a27f26.jpg)

3. When you are ready to make the new language(s) available to your users, click the **Republish** button in the top-right corner.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/d218f5bc145f8fec9d6c2e1d80bad723f53c211cafa6fdcd6ee060e1b701c340_256ed5da.jpg)

4. After publishing, a language selector will appear on your public knowledge base, allowing users to switch between the available languages.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/a7d0c521b5712f407d271f5f3fef7d81587cf77b2ec496c60927ddff84c6777d_ab088184.jpg)

## Manually Editing a Translation

While translations are automatic, you can override them with your own manual edits.

1. Select the translated language you wish to edit from the language dropdown.
2. Make your changes directly in the editor.
3. Click the **Save** button.
4. A confirmation pop-up will appear. Click **Save as Manual**. This action marks the translation as manual and prevents it from being automatically overwritten when the original article changes.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/71348ce3ce4a3f1cc4f8fd6cc26aaf5bd786b7f5874afe15cf65d856ff03cca0_1d6d9f26.jpg)

### Tips

If you need to revert a manual translation back to the auto-translated version, you can use **History** to restore a previous, automatically generated version of the article. This will re-enable automatic updates for that article.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/410c70d44862003b701156c12c14020c59918dee7b55cbf1757484071cc8935a_e8857ca4.jpg)

## Summary

This article teaches you how to add new languages to your knowledge base for automatic translation. The process involves selecting target languages in your project settings, allowing the system to translate the content, and then republishing to make the multilingual site live. This feature enables you to effortlessly support a global user base by providing documentation in their native language.

## Related

- [How to Publish Your Knowledge Base](#article-1002)
- [Customizing Your Knowledge Base](#article-1001)
- [Creating Your First Article](#article-1003)

---

### Understand Main vs. Translated Languages

<!-- Article URL: /docs/translating-and-localizing-content/understand-main-vs-translated-languages -->

# Understand Main vs. Translated Languages

Understanding the difference between **main and translated languages** is essential for managing your multilingual documentation efficiently in Hinto. The main language acts as the single source of truth for your content, while translated languages are the different versions you create to serve a global audience.

## What Main and Translated Languages Are

Your documentation project is organized around one main language and one or more translated languages. Each plays a distinct role in your content workflow.

### Main Language

The main language is the foundation of your project content. It is the original version from which all other languages are derived.

- **Source of Truth:** It's the primary version you write, edit, and maintain.
- **Translation Origin:** All automatic translations are generated from the content in your main language.
- **AI Features:** When using features like "Remake" to generate content, the AI uses the main language as its source.
- **Project Default:** This is typically the language you select when first creating your project, but it can be changed in your project settings.

### Translated Languages

Translated languages are the additional language versions of your documentation that you add to your project.

- **Automatic Generation:** They are created by automatically translating the content from your main language.
- **Independent Versions:** While they originate from the main language, each translated article can be edited manually if you need to refine the wording or terminology.
- **Global Reach:** Adding translated languages allows you to publish your documentation for different regions and audiences.

## Why This Distinction Matters

The main vs. translated language structure is designed to make managing multilingual documentation simple and scalable.

- **Maintain Consistency:** By having a single main language as the source of truth, you ensure that core information is consistent. When an update is needed, you only have to edit one article.
- **Improve Efficiency:** When you change an article in the main language, Hinto can automatically update all the corresponding translated versions, saving you significant time and effort.
- **Gain Full Control:** You have the flexibility to manually edit any auto-translated article. This allows you to correct nuances or use specific terminology that an automatic translation might miss, without altering the original text.

## How It Works: Auto vs. Manual Translations

By default, translated articles are set to update automatically. However, you can switch any article to manual control.

- **Auto-Translated (Default):** Any changes made to the main language article will be automatically reflected in its translated versions the next time content is updated.
- **Manual Translation:** If you directly edit and save a translated article, it becomes a "manual translation." This locks the article and prevents it from being automatically overwritten. This is useful for protecting specific, carefully crafted translations. You can always revert to an auto-translated version using the article's version history.

## Summary

The main language serves as the single, authoritative source for your documentation, from which all translated language versions are generated. This system enables efficient, automatic updates across multiple languages, ensuring consistency while still providing the flexibility to make manual edits. Understanding this structure helps you manage and scale your multilingual content effectively.

## Related

- [Translate Your Documentation](#article-8271)
- [Manage Article Version History](#article-3109)
- [Publish Your Documentation](#article-5543)

---

### How to Manually Edit and Manage Translated Articles

<!-- Article URL: /docs/translating-and-localizing-content/how-to-manually-edit-and-manage-translated-articles -->

# How to Manually Edit and Manage Translated Articles

This guide shows you how to make manual edits to an automatically translated article. Following these steps allows you to refine translations for clarity, context, or specific terminology, ensuring your content is perfect for every audience. You will need to have at least one translated language set up in your project.

1. Navigate to the article you wish to edit.
2. Click the **language selector** dropdown menu at the top of the editor.
3. Select the translated language you want to modify (e.g., French, Japanese). The page will reload to show the translated version of the article.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/a6c4bf5a333c819e94ea447bdee2d478f4f4f97385dfa3590507f7b380eef487_c4d69ef6.jpg)

4. Click directly on the text in the editor to make your desired changes. You can edit the title, headings, and body content.
5. Once you have finished editing, click the **Save** button.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/968b0b388e5fa9c2c2dbed3a0aef96a8ede15d5b3440ba98896b45cbc134f789_24db0433.jpg)

6. A "Save as manual translation?" pop-up will appear. This explains that saving a manual edit will prevent this specific article from being automatically updated in the future. Click **Save as Manual** to confirm.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/6270c2294446159eb912baa567546e8009669d9a4f98fa13d424a174cde4d033_96632098.jpg)

7. The editor will now label this article as a "Manual" translation instead of "Translation."

### Reverting to an Automatic Translation

If you want to discard your manual edits and return to the automatically translated version, you can use the version history.

1. With the manually edited article open, click the **History** button.
2. In the Version History panel, find and select a version that was created before your manual edit.
3. Click the **Restore vX** button at the bottom of the panel to revert the article. This will restore the auto-translated content and re-enable automatic updates.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/5faded5c61e90ca4522e57dd71d1898e532b0537099e2a60662a84045cae9942_2a29bb35.jpg)

## Summary

This article explains how to take control of your multilingual documentation by manually editing auto-translated articles. The process involves selecting the target language, making direct text edits, and saving the changes, which converts the article to a manual version. This feature provides the flexibility to refine machine translations for greater accuracy while also offering a simple way to revert to the automated version using the article's history.

## Related

- [Translating Your Documentation](#article-8951)
- [Publishing Your Documentation](#article-3301)
- [Managing Article Version History](#article-1250)

---

### How to Remove a Language from a Project

<!-- Article URL: /docs/translating-and-localizing-content/how-to-remove-a-language-from-a-project -->

# How to Remove a Language from a Project

This guide explains how to remove a translated language from your Hinto project. Following these steps will permanently delete a language and all of its associated translated articles. You will need access to your project's settings to complete this action.

1. From the article editor, click the language dropdown menu located in the top action bar.
2. Select **+ Add Languages** from the dropdown list to open the settings menu.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/bd2298770c39a6c2c9ae69f4551c98d59aec31559f1c225b134922a1fa145f40_63563a44.png)

3. In the **Project Settings** window, ensure you are on the **Languages** tab.
4. Under the Translated Languages heading, find the language you want to remove.
5. Click the **trash can** icon to the right of the language name.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/4e3d94e2d13142d79555dceffdc4f1a273fc02ed41878ec044643479e4bbd36b_37824116.jpg)

6. A confirmation window will appear to prevent accidental deletion. Click the **Delete** button to permanently remove the language.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/158ff0c1eeec7a3a274f557ea0a10538cf93d61dbd0ab846fbe62b87053def42_d50025a2.jpg)

### Important Considerations

Please note that deleting a language is a permanent action and cannot be undone. All translated content for that specific language will be removed from your project. If you need to add the language again in the future, you will have to repeat the translation process.

## Summary

This article provides a step-by-step guide on how to remove a translated language from a project. By accessing the Languages tab within Project Settings, you can select and permanently delete any translated language, which removes all of its content. This helps you manage which languages are available in your published documentation.

## Related

- [How to Add and Translate Languages in a Project](#article-1101)
- [Setting Your Project's Main Language](#article-1102)
- [How to Publish Your Project](/docs/publishing-documentation/how-to-publish-your-project-with-a-public-url)

### How to Configure SEO Settings for Translated Articles

<!-- Article URL: /docs/translating-and-localizing-content/how-to-configure-seo-settings-for-translated-articles -->

# How to Configure SEO Settings for Translated Articles

This guide shows you how to customize SEO settings for different language versions of your articles. Following these steps allows you to optimize the URL slug, meta description, and keywords for each translation, improving search engine performance in local markets. You will need an article that has already been translated into one or more languages.

1. Navigate to the article you want to configure.
2. Click the **three-dot menu** icon located next to the **Save** button.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/73621958ecc6f01224dbb60466c930edb3178a456c3895e4f3a9b677a01c84bf_aba1ad51.jpg)

3. Select **Settings** from the dropdown menu.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/4884d123193ee3882f88130c83ed9516832068a381c93f3d55be43f590c8d489_78295402.jpg)

4. In the **Settings** window, click the tab corresponding to the language you wish to edit (e.g., **French**).
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/aef8411db9b78a118c1589d775331fd1ea0c01df1588246ee8d8551cf10e5dae_048638f5.jpg)

5. Ensure the **Enable translation** toggle is switched on. If you are re-enabling a translation, you may be prompted to keep the existing content or retranslate it from scratch.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/ee87311fbbc18b2241dbc3765b8d67bf137e04d9827996be3511f356b6905de5_29208b7b.jpg)

6. Update the **URL Slug**, **Meta Description**, and **Keywords** to optimize them for the selected language.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/e18610925c6afa267a1ecd48da9d18292a07f9d3b9076ca04d9a182e12ce57dc_40ff2056.jpg)

7. Click the **Save Settings** button.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/ac90511ec32d4f4263a63c9ba17bae213bb21021c8ad50398494a53909eb6d78_14a51b99.jpg)

8. Click **Republish** at the top of the page to make your changes live.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/269a95ab638d47e57a201debcb6a0185bdc9cf0d062c09158ec35df3e9d38a50_6e953c45.jpg)

## Summary

This article provides a step-by-step walkthrough on how to configure unique SEO settings for each translated version of your content. By customizing the URL slug, meta description, and keywords for different languages, you can enhance your article's visibility and relevance in regional search engine results, ensuring it effectively reaches a global audience.

## Related

- [How to Add and Manage Languages](#article-tl02)
- [How to Enable and Disable Translations for an Article](#article-tl03)
- [Understanding SEO Settings](#article-sp01)

---

### How to Enable or Disable a Translated Article

<!-- Article URL: /docs/translating-and-localizing-content/how-to-enable-or-disable-a-translated-article -->

# How to Enable or Disable a Translated Article

This guide explains how to enable or disable specific language versions of your articles. Following these steps allows you to control which translations are visible to your audience, which is useful when content or features are not available in all regions. You will need an article that has already been translated.

1. Navigate to the article for which you want to manage a translation.
2. In the top-right corner of the editor, click the **...** (More options) icon.
3. Select **Settings** from the dropdown menu.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/0f6a1cb8af7977cb0e55560610f7bcd0f346849ba93686b20f570b59b3638ecf_c0447858.jpg)

4. In the **Settings** window, select the tab for the language you want to manage (e.g., **French**).
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/0d7ab199fc36097743fca68f0678e4ebfbedfe9337ec5aa766821d9e5d382f49_f1e5575a.jpg)

5. Click the **Enable translation** toggle to change its status.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/d18b4580476dadf54e1e37bf0d209ee00826c41053d19fdd7423d55fa956b17e_67158f5e.jpg)

6. If you are re-enabling a translation that was previously disabled, a "Re-enable translation" window will appear. Choose one of the following options:
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/ee87311fbbc18b2241dbc3765b8d67bf137e04d9827996be3511f356b6905de5_29208b7b.jpg)

7. Click the **Save Settings** button to apply your changes.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/ac90511ec32d4f4263a63c9ba17bae213bb21021c8ad50398494a53909eb6d78_14a51b99.jpg)

8. Click the **Republish** button at the top of the page to make your changes live.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/269a95ab638d47e57a201debcb6a0185bdc9cf0d062c09158ec35df3e9d38a50_6e953c45.jpg)

## Summary

This article demonstrates how to manage the visibility of your translated articles. By accessing the language-specific settings, you can enable or disable individual translations, providing granular control over your multilingual content. This functionality is essential for ensuring that only relevant and available information is published for each specific language audience.

## Related

- [Translating Articles into Multiple Languages](#article-pub-02)
- [How to Publish Your Articles](#article-pub-01)
- [Viewing and Managing Article History](#article-pub-03)

---

### Add Translation Rules for Specific Terminology

<!-- Article URL: /docs/translating-and-localizing-content/add-translation-rules-for-specific-terminology -->

# Add Translation Rules for Specific Terminology

This guide shows you how to add translation rules to ensure your specific product names, acronyms, and terminology are translated consistently and accurately across different languages. By creating a glossary of terms, you can improve the quality of your localized documentation. You will need to have at least one translated language already added to your project.

1. From your project, click **Settings** in the top navigation bar.
2. In the Project Settings menu, select the **Languages** tab from the left-hand sidebar.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/0a33f8912c88d99cf832d09ba6846e8694804a0f8c2cfa83c563d8b08168c0dd_384c4a64.png)

3. Locate the language you want to add rules for under the Translated languages list.
4. Click the **Add rules** or **Edit rules** button next to the corresponding language.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/5ddd5f6716f7e0f33ae01a665f8c00bf9e88a8cae9ed93f0ce1d86fb4ea4ab87_7fe80523.png)

5. In the Translation Rules modal, enter your specific terms. Add one rule per line with the original term in the main language, followed by a space or tab, and then the correct translation.
6. Click **Save** to confirm your rules.
![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/7d9a5515feeb5509cd4ab7ca3b9a7384b916bacdac8ef69d2345feb88e34d078_92eef18a.png)

7. After saving, the rules will automatically apply to any new content you generate. To apply these rules to all existing articles in that language, click the **Re-translate** button next to the language.

![](https://otegsqwqycxxjehewzxi.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/published-media/ca741c6f-de5a-493a-aed0-e6b2eaa7248e/media/46d633bdb7f17dd6f21806a8d83528baefd91fb18191561247f729c5c370cba4_b9245157.png)

### Tips

For best results, prepare your terminology list in a spreadsheet with two columns (one for the original term, one for the translation). You can then copy and paste the entire list directly into the **Translation Rules** field.

## Summary

Adding translation rules allows you to create a project-specific glossary to maintain brand consistency and technical accuracy across multiple languages. This feature gives you control over how key terms are localized, ensuring that all automatically translated content uses your preferred phrasing. Once rules are set, you can re-translate existing content to apply the updates retroactively.
